title: AI Adoption Readiness

introduction.subtitle: Transformation readiness assessment. 6 min.
introduction.description:
AI Adoption Readiness helps you learn the vocabulary and typical expectations behind Use Cases & Value, Data & Technology, People & Workflow, Governance & Measurement. Use it as a private diagnostic: answer from observable evidence, not aspiration, and treat the result as a map for the next improvement cycle.

The score stays separate from the optional answer-support check, which asks whether your answers are based on opinion, anecdotes, documentation, or system-verified evidence. A high answer should mean the practice is visible, repeatable, and useful in real decisions.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.duration: 8 diagnostic questions plus 4 evidence checks. Approximately 6 min.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How are AI use cases selected?

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Use Cases & Value; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in Use Cases & Value, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. Use Cases & Value has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.
D. Use Cases & Value is reviewed, measured over time, and strong enough to guide trade-offs under pressure.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Use Cases & Value: How are AI use cases selected
conceptHelp.summary: Use Cases & Value means selecting AI work by business value, user need, feasibility, risk, and measurable outcome instead of by novelty or internal enthusiasm. This question narrows the concept to "How are AI use cases selected?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Use Cases & Value matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show the current AI practice for "How are AI use cases selected?", with owners, controls, evidence, and a recent deployment, monitoring, or rejection decision.

Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How ready are the relevant data and knowledge sources?

options.text: A. The information is fragmented, missing, or too inconsistent to support decisions.
B. Partial tools or reports exist, but definitions and ownership are weak.
C. Definitions, owners, access rules, and regular review are governed.
D. Lineage, quality controls, exception handling, and a feedback loop into decisions exist.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Data & Technology: How ready are the relevant data and knowledge sources
conceptHelp.summary: Data & Technology means the readiness of data, knowledge sources, architecture, security, and integration needed for AI to work reliably in production. This question narrows the concept to "How ready are the relevant data and knowledge sources?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

options.text: A. Authority is unclear, improvised, or concentrated in one person.
B. The practice is informal; boundaries, escalation, or records are inconsistent.
C. Owners, decision rules, and review points are documented for important cases.
D. Authority, evidence standards, exception handling, and periodic review are governed.
Not sure / not applicable.

contextQuestions.text: What level of evidence supports your answers for Use Cases & Value?

options.text: Opinion: mostly judgement, memory, or individual perception.
Anecdotal: examples exist, but they are not systematically documented.
Documented: there are current notes, models, policies, metrics, or records.
System-verified: evidence is routinely produced by a governed workflow, system, or review cadence.

conceptHelp.title: Use Cases & Value
conceptHelp.summary: This question asks how well your answers about Use Cases & Value are supported. Use Cases & Value means selecting AI work by business value, user need, feasibility, risk, and measurable outcome instead of by novelty or internal enthusiasm. The goal is learning: separate what you know from what you suspect, remember, or still need to verify.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: A result about Use Cases & Value is more useful when you know whether it rests on opinion, a few examples, current documents, or system-verified evidence. Weak support does not make the answer useless; it tells you what to learn next.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Choose the strongest support you actually have right now. Do not upgrade an answer because the practice feels important, and do not downgrade it if current records or systems really exist.
conceptHelp.example: If your Use Cases & Value answer is based on a recent metric, policy, deal document, customer record, or operating review, choose documented or system-verified. If it is mostly memory or judgement, choose opinion or anecdotal.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
contextDescription: This step is not scored. It records what your diagnostic answers are based on: opinion, anecdote, documentation, or system-verified evidence. Use it to decide how much to verify the result before acting on it.
